title: Memory System Mastery
description: Master MEMORY.md, daily notes, and how to make your agent remember everything that matters.
content_mdx:
# Memory System Mastery

OpenClaw's memory system is one of its most powerful features. It makes your agent feel like a true personal assistant — one that actually remembers your life, not just the last 10 messages.

## The Memory Architecture

OpenClaw uses a two-tier memory system:

**Tier 1: Long-term Memory (MEMORY.md)**
- Curated, important facts about you and your life
- Manually curated or agent-managed
- Always injected into every conversation

**Tier 2: Daily Notes (memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md)**
- Raw conversation summaries and events
- Created automatically by your agent
- Searchable and reviewable

## MEMORY.md Structure

Location: `~/.openclaw/workspace/MEMORY.md`

```markdown
# MEMORY.md - Long-Term Memory

Last Updated: 2026-03-05

## Quick Reference
- Currently building ClawMaster app (SvelteKit + Supabase)
- Team: Ivan (frontend), Pavel (QA), Misha (ops)
- Main AI model: Claude Sonnet 4 via OpenRouter
- Goal: Launch by end of March 2026

## About Me
- CEO of automated mobile dev company
- Working from Sofia, Bulgaria (GMT+2)
- Prefer direct communication, no fluff
- Currently focusing on AI-powered workflows

## Active Projects
### ClawMaster
- Status: In development
- Stack: SvelteKit, Tailwind, Supabase, Vercel
- Target: $60k MRR from AI learning platform

### Mobile Dev Company
- Automated app development pipeline
- Target: 50 apps by Q2 2026

## Key Decisions & Learnings
- Always use coordinator pattern for multi-step pipelines
- Use Supabase SSR for auth, not client-side only
- OpenRouter > direct provider APIs for flexibility
```

## How Memory Works

Every conversation:
1. MEMORY.md is injected into the system prompt
2. The conversation happens
3. At the end (or on request), the agent updates daily notes
4. Important items get curated into MEMORY.md

## Telling Your Agent to Remember Things

Simply say it in conversation:

```
Remember that I switched from PostgreSQL to Supabase for this project.
```

Or explicitly:
```
Add this to memory: My deployment target is Vercel, not AWS.
```

Or the command:
```
/remember I'm using TypeScript strict mode for this project
```

## Daily Notes

Location: `~/.openclaw/workspace/memory/`

Format: `YYYY-MM-DD.md`

```markdown
# 2026-03-05

## Conversations
- Discussed ClawMaster architecture with agent
- Decided to use Supabase for auth (not custom JWT)
- Agent helped debug the hooks.server.ts issue

## Decisions Made
- PayPal over Stripe (simpler for one-time payments)
- Tailwind over plain CSS

## Tasks Completed
- Auth system implemented
- Landing page built
- 15 module content written
```

## Searching Memory

```bash
# Search all memory and notes:
openclaw memory search "supabase"

# View recent notes:
openclaw memory recent

# View a specific date:
openclaw memory show 2026-03-01
```

## Agent-Managed Memory

If your agent has the memory skill enabled, it will:

- Automatically identify important information to remember
- Update MEMORY.md with significant events
- Create daily notes summaries
- Answer questions about past conversations

## AGENTS.md — System Instructions

There's a third memory file: `AGENTS.md`. This contains:

- Quick start instructions for new sessions
- Active project context
- Team and infrastructure info
- Rules the agent must always follow

Think of AGENTS.md as your agent's "briefing document" — operational instructions that never change.

```markdown
# AGENTS.md - Your Workspace

## Every Session - Quick Start
1. Read SOUL.md
2. Read USER.md
3. Check MEMORY.md for current context

## Active Projects
- ClawMaster: SvelteKit app at /workspace/clawmaster
- Task Master: Node.js app at /workspace/task-master

## Rules
- Always commit code after major changes
- Use coordinator pattern for multi-step pipelines
- Test before deploying
```

## Memory Best Practices

- **Keep MEMORY.md concise.** It's injected every conversation — too long wastes tokens.
- **Update regularly.** Set a weekly reminder to review and prune.
- **Use Quick Reference section.** Put the most-needed facts at the top.
- **Date entries.** Know when you wrote something so you can judge its relevance.

## Viewing Your Agent's Memory From Chat

```
/memory
```

Shows the current contents of MEMORY.md in the conversation.

```
/memory update
```

Triggers the agent to update MEMORY.md based on recent conversations.
